description: Single-machine Go CLI that manages config for AI coding agents (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex, and more) from one canonical source, rendered into each agent's native format by `agentsync apply`.
rules: agentsync manages a canonical source under ~/.agentsync/ (project scope: a .agentsync/ tree at the repo root). `agentsync apply` renders it into each agent's native config — never hand-edit rendered native files; edit the canonical source and re-apply.
It is bidirectional: native edits are detected as drift and folded back into the canonical source via `reconcile`/`import`, never overwritten silently.
Secrets are referenced as ${secret:...} / ${env:...} and resolved at apply time from an age-encrypted vault. Cleartext secrets are never persisted back into the canonical source.
`apply` is offline and deterministic — it renders from a local cache and never touches the network; only `update` reaches out.
Per-agent, per-component support varies — consult the capability matrix. Coverage that can't be fully represented is reported (lossy ◐) or skipped (✗), never dropped silently.
